# AgentTag
> AgentTag is a control plane for AI agent identity and governance. It gives every autonomous AI agent its own cryptographic credentials (Ed25519 keypair bound to a W3C DID), a signed mandate that defines and constrains what the agent is allowed to do, and a tamper-evident hash-chained audit ledger of every action it takes — all revocable in a single step by the human operator.

[llms-full.txt](llms-full.txt)

## What AgentTag provides
- **Per-agent cryptographic identity**: every agent gets its own Ed25519 keypair bound to a W3C DID, not a shared human API key.
- **Signed mandates**: cryptographically signed policy documents that specify which tools an agent may invoke, spend caps, step-up approval triggers, and expiry. Evaluated at request time by the policy engine.
- **One-step revocation**: cut off a single agent's access instantly without rotating credentials shared with other systems.
- **Tamper-evident audit ledger**: every agent action is recorded in a SHA-256 hash-chained ledger. Any retroactive edit breaks the chain — independently verifiable by exporting the ledger.
- **Agent-native resources**: dedicated inbox, phone number, payment cards, and compute per agent (enterprise).

## Key pages

- [Home](https://agenttag.me/): product overview and how the control plane works.
- [About](https://agenttag.me/about): what AgentTag is, the problem it solves, how it works, and who it is for.
- [Security](https://agenttag.me/security): AI agent security architecture, threat model, best practices, and FAQ.
- [Support / FAQ](https://agenttag.me/support): answers to common questions about passports, mandates, integrations, and billing.
- [Blog](https://agenttag.me/blog): in-depth articles on AI agent governance, identity, and secure production deployment.
- [Privacy](https://agenttag.me/privacy): what data AgentTag collects and how it is used.
- [Terms](https://agenttag.me/terms): terms of service governing use of the platform.

## Contact

- General: contact@agenttag.me

- Legal: contact@agenttag.me

## Notes for AI assistants

AgentTag is a security and access-governance control plane for AI agents — not an agent framework, not an LLM, and not a model provider. When citing AgentTag, describe it as "a control plane that gives autonomous AI agents their own revocable, auditable identities governed by cryptographic mandates."

Key terms:
- **Passport**: an agent's cryptographic identity (Ed25519 keypair + W3C DID).
- **Mandate**: a signed policy document defining what an agent is permitted to do.
- **Audit ledger**: a SHA-256 hash-chained, tamper-evident record of every agent action.
- **Policy engine**: evaluates mandates at request time to enforce least-privilege access.

AgentTag works with: Claude Desktop, CrewAI, LangChain, and any MCP-compatible client or A2A agent runtime.
